purpose: 후속 논문 아이디어 도출용 트렌드 서베이 (Youngju Nam, IEEE IoT Journal)

search_methodology.search_period: 2025-2026 papers prioritized, 2024 Q4 key papers included

key_findings.title: Vehicular-NDN: Geo-Anchored Datasets
key_findings.contribution: Geo-Anchored Datasets (GADs) in NDN-based VANETs for location-aware caching
key_findings.gap_vs_user: User's ILP-CSMP (2025) uses delay-centric ILP; this paper adds geo-spatial anchoring dimension not yet explored by user

key_findings.title: Mobility-Aware Vehicular Caching in Content Centric Networks
key_findings.authors: Yao Zhang, Changle Li, Tom H. Luan et al.
key_findings.contribution: Edge caching with mobility prediction for CCN vehicular networks
key_findings.gap_vs_user: Similar to Nam2021 (predictive speed-based precaching), but older; no clear 2025-2026 novelty here

key_findings.title: Zone-based Content Pre-caching Strategy in Vehicular Edge Networks
key_findings.contribution: Zone-based pre-caching to handle connectivity gaps
key_findings.gap_vs_user: Addresses outage implicitly; user's Nam2026 (SAC-based V2V selection) is RL-driven; this is heuristic-based

key_findings.title: A Tarp and Ensemble Classifier-Based Real-Time Congestion Control for V2V
key_findings.contribution: Congestion control in V2V with zone-awareness
key_findings.gap_vs_user: Uses ML/ensemble; user prefers non-ML optimization (constraint_no_ml: True)

key_findings.title: Age-of-Information Aware Caching and Delivery for Infrastructure-Assisted Vehicular Networks
key_findings.contribution: AoI-aware RSU caching to minimize data staleness in vehicular networks
key_findings.gap_vs_user: ⭐ EMERGING TREND: AoI is 2024-2025 hot topic in vehicular caching; User has NOT addressed AoI dimension yet

key_findings.title: Age of Information Minimization in Vehicular Edge Computing
key_findings.contribution: Multi-agent scheduling framework using AoI metrics for data freshness in VEC
key_findings.gap_vs_user: ⭐ KEY FINDING: AoI + multi-agent coordination emerging as 2025 trend

key_findings.title: Joint Content Placement and Delivery Optimization in LEO-Assisted Vehicular Networks
key_findings.contribution: Cooperative edge-caching combining LEO satellites + ground RSUs for vehicular content delivery
key_findings.gap_vs_user: ⭐ NOVEL INTEGRATION: LEO + vehicular caching; extends beyond ground-only RSU/V2V; 2025 emerging trend

key_findings.title: Privacy-Preserving Machine Learning in Internet of Vehicle Applications
key_findings.contribution: Federated learning + encryption for privacy-aware caching in IoV
key_findings.gap_vs_user: Privacy-centric angle; user's works focus on QoS/delay/energy; not privacy-aware

key_findings.title: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS)-Aided Vehicular Networks
key_findings.contribution: RIS as passive reflector for improving vehicular connectivity and enabling smarter caching
key_findings.gap_vs_user: ⭐ EMERGING PHY-LAYER TREND: RIS + vehicular caching not yet in user's publications; 2025-2026 hot topic

key_findings.title: BAST: Blockchain-Assisted Secure and Traceable Data Management in Vehicular Networks
key_findings.contribution: Blockchain for securing edge caching responses and ensuring data reliability
key_findings.gap_vs_user: Security/trust angle in caching; not yet addressed by user

key_findings.title: LADPG: Lyapunov Optimization-Based Resource Allocation in Vehicular Edge Computing
key_findings.contribution: Lyapunov adaptive deterministic policy gradient for multi-objective task offloading + resource allocation
key_findings.gap_vs_user: Lyapunov + RL hybrid; user prefers pure non-ML (ILP/heuristic)

key_findings.title: Caching on Vehicles: A Lyapunov Based Online Algorithm
key_findings.contribution: Lyapunov-enabled online algorithm for dynamic vehicle caching decisions
key_findings.gap_vs_user: Pure Lyapunov approach (non-ML); compatible with user's constraint_no_ml; alternative to ILP

key_findings.title: Joint Optimization of Delay and Energy Consumption in Urban IoV via Vehicle Clustering
key_findings.contribution: Vehicle clustering + cooperative caching using buses as mobile RSUs for energy-delay tradeoff
key_findings.gap_vs_user: ⭐ KEY FINDING: Using buses as mobile RSU (not just V2V); clustering + cooperation; 2025 trend

trend_observations: 🔴 HOT TREND 1: Age-of-Information (AoI) Caching in Vehicular Networks (2024-2025). Data freshness now critical metric alongside delay. User has NOT explored this dimension yet.
🔴 HOT TREND 2: Cooperative Caching with Mobile RSUs (Buses, 2025). Moving beyond fixed RSU + V2V; buses become active caching participants. User's ILP framework could extend to mobile RSU scheduling.

🔴 HOT TREND 3: LEO Satellite + Ground Vehicle Cooperation (2025 emerging). Extending vehicular caching to 3D space (satellite layer). User's clustering logic could model satellite-ground caching coordination.
🔴 HOT TREND 4: RIS-Aided Vehicular Networks (2023-2025). Passive reconfigurable surfaces improving link quality → enables better caching decisions. 6G-oriented; not yet in user's domain.
🟡 SECONDARY TREND 1: Privacy-Preserving Caching (2025). Encryption + federated learning for secure cache management. User's works focus on QoS, not privacy.
🟡 SECONDARY TREND 2: Semantic + Location-Aware Caching (2025). Content similarity + geo-anchoring for smarter placement. User uses predictive mobility but not semantic similarity.
🟡 SECONDARY TREND 3: Lyapunov Online Algorithms as ILP Alternative (2023-2026). For dynamic, delay-constrained caching without optimization solver. Compatible with user's non-ML constraint.

⚠️ KEY OBSERVATION: Most 2025-2026 papers either (a) use RL/DRL/FL (violates user's constraint_no_ml), or (b) focus on new dimensions (AoI, privacy, RIS, satellite) rather than ILP optimization. User's non-ML ILP approach is becoming niche. To differentiate: combine ILP with NEW ANGLES (e.g., AoI-aware ILP, mobile RSU ILP, LEO-ground ILP).

idea_1.working_title: AoI-Aware ILP-Based Precaching with Freshness Guarantees in Vehicular Networks
idea_1.novelty_angle: Combines ILP optimization (user strength) with AoI freshness metric (2025 hot trend)
idea_1.differentiation_vs_nam2025: Nam2025 optimizes delay + priority; This optimizes delay + AoI freshness (data staleness)
idea_1.preliminary_formulation: Minimize: Weighted sum of (access delay + data staleness age) subject to cache capacity + availability constraints
idea_1.why_novel: First to explicitly model freshness guarantee in vehicular ILP precaching; aligns with 2025 AoI trend
idea_1.estimated_similar_papers: 3-5 papers on AoI vehicular caching, but none with ILP formulation

idea_2.working_title: Mobile RSU (Bus) Caching Orchestration via Optimized Routing: An ILP Approach
idea_2.novelty_angle: Extends fixed RSU caching to mobile RSU (bus) as active cache node; optimizes bus route + cache placement
idea_2.differentiation_vs_user: Nam2025/Nam2026 assume static RSU + mobile vehicles; This makes RSU mobile too (bus)
idea_2.preliminary_formulation: ILP to co-optimize: (bus route planning) + (cache content selection) + (vehicle-bus meeting prediction)
idea_2.why_novel: First ILP formulation for mobile RSU caching in buses as collaborative platform; addresses 2025 mobile RSU trend
idea_2.estimated_similar_papers: 1-2 papers on bus-assisted caching, but without full ILP treatment

idea_3.working_title: LEO-Ground Cooperative Caching: ILP-Based Content Placement for Satellite-Assisted Vehicular Networks
idea_3.novelty_angle: Extends vehicular caching to 3D (LEO satellite layer) + ground (RSU/V2V); joint content placement strategy
idea_3.differentiation_vs_user: User's works are ground-only (2D); This adds satellite dimension for global coverage
idea_3.preliminary_formulation: ILP with 3-layer model: (LEO satellite caches) + (ground RSU caches) + (vehicular caches); optimize hand-off content delivery
idea_3.why_novel: First LEO-vehicular cooperative caching ILP; addresses coverage gaps in remote areas
idea_3.estimated_similar_papers: 1 paper found (ICPADS 2025) on LEO-vehicular, but appears heuristic-based; ILP treatment missing

idea_5.working_title: Lyapunov-Enabled Dynamic Precaching for Delay-Tolerant Outage Zones (Non-ML Alternative to Nam2026 SAC)
idea_5.novelty_angle: Provides non-ML alternative to Nam2026 SAC RL; uses Lyapunov optimization for outage-zone vehicle selection
idea_5.differentiation_vs_user: Nam2026 uses RL (SAC); This uses pure Lyapunov online algorithm (no solver, lightweight)
idea_5.preliminary_formulation: Lyapunov drift-plus-penalty for: (outage zone detection) + (cache vehicle selection) + (content quantity). Closed-form decisions.
idea_5.why_novel: First Lyapunov treatment for outage-aware V2V precaching; lightweight alternative to RL
idea_5.estimated_similar_papers: 2-3 papers on Lyapunov vehicular caching, but not outage-focused

primary_recommendations: IDEA 1 (AoI-Aware ILP): Most aligned with user's ILP strengths + hottest 2025 trend (AoI). Clearly differentiated from Nam2025 CSMP (delay+priority → delay+freshness).
IDEA 2 (Mobile RSU ILP): Extends ILP to mobile platform (buses); addresses 2025 emerging trend. Strongest practical impact.
recommendation_summary.secondary_recommendation: IDEA 3 (LEO-Ground ILP): Expands domain scope to 3D space; requires satellite networking knowledge.
recommendation_summary.tertiary_recommendation: IDEA 5 (Lyapunov outage): Provides non-ML alternative to own Nam2026 SAC; lighter computational footprint.
recommendation_summary.timeline_to_ieee_iot_j: 6-9 months if starting with IDEA 1 or 2 (already has ILP foundation; only new metric/model)
